title: Smoky Roasted Thai Eggplant Yum Salad
description: A smoky and refreshing Thai salad featuring roasted Thai eggplant, perfect for a hangover cure or a light lunch. This dish is shojin-friendly and served in a lacquerware bowl.

ingredients.name: fish-free sauce
ingredients.notes: such as nam pla or tamari

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the Thai eggplant into 1-inch pieces and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the vegetable oil over the eggplant and sprinkle with smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Roast the eggplant in the oven for 20 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
Remove the eggplant from the oven and let cool to room temperature.
In a large bowl, whisk together the lime juice and fish-free sauce.
Add the cooled eggplant, chopped cilantro, crushed peanuts, and diced red onion to the bowl and toss to combine.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the salad in a lacquerware bowl and garnish with additional cilantro if desired.
